title: Mean, Variance, and Covariance
learningObjectives: Understand that zero covariance does not mean independence

whyThisLesson.text: To analyze large sets of activations, we need statistical tools to summarize their distributions and relationships.

intuition.analogy: Mean is the center of gravity. Variance is how spread out the cloud is. Covariance is whether the cloud tilts diagonally.
intuition.text: Statistics of vector collections. Cov=0 does NOT mean independent. Preview: X~Uniform(-1,1), Y=X² has Cov=0 but Y is determined by X.

visual.question: How does covariance affect the scatter plot?
visual.description: Scatter plots showing positive, negative, and zero covariance.

technical.text: Covariance measures linear joint variability. Zero covariance implies no linear correlation, but non-linear dependencies can still exist.

definitions.definition: A matrix containing covariances between all pairs of dimensions.

evidence.label: [Non-linear dependence]
evidence.paraphrase: Variables can have perfect non-linear dependence while having zero covariance.

failureModes.name: Assuming independence from zero covariance
failureModes.mitigation: Use mutual information or other metrics.

exercises.prompt: What is the mean of (1,2) and (3,4)?

masteryQuestions.prompt: Give an example of zero covariance with full dependence.
rubric: Must mention a symmetric non-linear function.
masteryQuestions.goodVersion: X uniformly distributed around 0, Y = X^2. They are perfectly dependent but covariance is zero.

bridgeToNext.text: Now we use covariance matrices to find principal directions.
